title: Factories building the factory docs
description: A grounded examination of how agent-factory workflows—planner/executor lanes, harness loops, worktrees, and review—were used to build the you-agent-factory documentation site.
contextSentence: This documentation site was not authored as a one-shot chat dump. It was built with the same agent-factory patterns the product teaches: persistent work queues, harness-driven loops, isolated worktrees, and review before merge.
takeaway: Treat large docs work as a factory workload: plan and dispatch small vertical pages, keep harness loops and worktree isolation honest, and link claims back to published concepts and techniques instead of inventing a second glossary in the blog.
